narrative:
# Market Intelligence Briefing | 21 May 2026

European equity markets remain in a benign regime with only 1 of 11 sectors in negative territory, supported by a stress score of 18/100 and VIX at 17.4 (42nd percentile of the 10-year range). Credit spreads are tightening across both segments—HY OAS at 279bp (14th percentile) and IG OAS at 76bp—indicating investor confidence is outpacing fundamentals, with z-scores of -0.96 and -1.12 respectively signaling overvaluation relative to historical norms. The VIX term structure remains in contango and no panic signals have been triggered, though the absence of Type B dislocations and Type C deep value opportunities suggests limited tactical entry points at current levels. Market breadth and credit technicals point to complacency rather than opportunity; position cautiously until either spreads widen toward mean levels or earnings data forces reassessment of embedded risk premiums.
